summary: Clear soundboard recording of the March 14 1990 Cap Centre show, highlighted by a scorching first set featuring Loose Lucy and Let It Grow.
review: The SBD capture is generally clean with only faint tape‑hiss between songs, letting the band’s peak spring‑90 energy shine through.  Reviewers rave about the opening set – Cold Rain & Snow, The Stranger, Row Jimmy and especially the explosive Loose Lucy that sent the crowd into a frenzy – while Let It Grow tops the first‑set climax.  The second set is solid with Crazy Fingers → PITB → U.J.B. highlights but receives fewer accolades than the opener.

ai_rating.rationale: Over two dozen detailed reviews overwhelmingly praise the performance and note good‑to‑excellent soundboard quality; only a few low‑star comments mention minor setlist preferences, giving a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear SBD mix with strong crowd presence; slight tape hiss between songs but no major drop‑outs or distortion. Listeners note the recording captures the venue’s energy well.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set launches hot and stays high‑energy, building to the peak of Loose Lucy and Let It Grow.  Second set is competent with a notable Crazy Fingers → PITB → U.J.B. sequence but feels more of a follow‑up than a second climax.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers cite Jerry’s “furious leads”, high‑on‑the‑fret‑board bends, and overall scorching solo work.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for intricate bass lines that weave in and out of the jams (e.g., “Phil runs in and out of his guitar‑work with intricate execution”).
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard work receives glowing remarks – “tearing sh*t up”, “shines throughout”, and especially strong piano on Big River and Good Times Blues.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming is noted for the sizzling second‑set drum solo that fuels the PITB → U.J.B. transition.
